betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Rocket Seat§f is a §6single block§f placed at the top of every AR rocket. It defines the §6passenger capacity§f, §6control surface§f, and §6inventory§f that the pilot uses during flight. The seat is a non-multiblock block that becomes a §6TileEntity§f only after the rocket spawns, with vanilla §6minecraft:wool§f used to color the seat cushion.

§6Production Path (libVulpes RecipesMachine)§f:
- Built via §6Crafting Table§f: 1x §6Wool§f (any of minecraft:wool:0-15 for cushion color) + 1x §6Steel Plate§f (libvulpes:productplate:6)
- Each AR rocket needs at least one §6Seat§f to be considered valid by the §6Rocket Assembling Machine§f (10022)
- The seat also stores §6inventory items§f for the pilot: oxygen, food, and tools are drawn from the seat's slot during flight

The seat has §6no direct crafting recipe§f in the AR source or CT scripts; it is registered at runtime through the libVulpes machine recipe system. Each rocket frame must contain at least one §6Seat§f placed at the top of the structure, and a rocket can have up to §6six seats§f for a multiplayer crew.

The seat is a prerequisite for the §6Guidance Computer§f (10028) and the §6Space Suit Chest-Piece§f chain. It also gates every §6planetary mission§f because rockets without a seat are flagged "unmanned" by the assembler and can only be flown by an §6Unmanned Vehicle Assembler§f (chain).

§eTip§f: Place the seat at the §6highest Y level§f of the rocket frame (above all fuel tanks, motors, and structural blocks). The assembler will reject the rocket if the seat is buried inside the structure rather than on top.
